channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Former Dolphins player Reshad Jones awarded settlement in fraud case
item.description: Former Miami Dolphins safety Reshad Jones settled a case against Merrill Lynch for $9.5 million over claims his former financial adviser committed theft.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Valentin Vacherot stuns Novak Djokovic and could face cousin in Shanghai Masters final
item.description: SHANGHAI (AP) — Valentin Vacherot earned the biggest win of his career by beating a hobbled Novak Djokovic 6-3, 6-4 on Saturday to reach the Shanghai Masters final — where he could face his cousin. The 204th-ranked Vacherot, of Monaco, was an alternate in qualifying but is having a career week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tarik Skubal turns in a dominant performance in Detroit's Game 5 loss to Mariners
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Tarik Skubal was terrific. And he was long gone when the season ended for the Detroit Tigers. Skubal struck out 13 in six dominant innings in Game 5 of the AL Division Series at Seattle on Friday night. But the Tigers were eliminated when Jorge Polanco singled home J.P.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Albert Pujols has met with the Angels about their managerial opening, AP source says
item.description: ANAHEIM, Calif. (AP) — Albert Pujols has met with the Los Angeles Angels about returning to their dugout as their next manager, a person with knowledge of the meeting told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Phillies face an uncertain future following their 4th straight playoff exit
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Orion Kerkering airmailed a throw into the Los Angeles night that not only unimaginably ended the Phillies’ season — it all but ended the run of the core of a roster that suffered through four straight postseasons without a World Series title.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-Celtics star Paul Pierce found asleep in car, arrested on suspicion of DUI
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former NBA player Paul Pierce was arrested Tuesday night on suspicion of driving under the influence on a Los Angeles highway after he was found asleep behind the wheel, state police said. California Highway Patrol officers responded at about 10:40 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clemson's Howard's Rock, 'Play Like a Champion Today' signs among beloved totems in college football
item.description: CLEMSON, S.C. (AP) — Hunter Renfrow snagged the game-winning touchdown in the College Football Playoff national championship game, was selected in the fifth round of the NFL draft and even made the Pro Bowl.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philadelphia suffers miserable sports night in city's first 'triple loss' in over four decades
item.description: Philadelphia sports fans suffered a rare blow as the Phillies, Eagles and Flyers all lost on the same night — the first time that’s happened since 1983.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Manchester is saying its last goodbye to boxing icon Ricky Hatton
item.description: MANCHESTER, England (AP) — Manchester is saying its last goodbye to boxing icon Ricky Hatton. The former world champion died last month aged 46. His funeral was being held Friday in the city’s cathedral and fans lined up as the cortège made its way through the streets.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ian Happ's homer sets the tone for the Cubs in a playoff victory against the Brewers
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — With one big swing, Ian Happ sent a charge through an electric crowd of 41,770 at Wrigley Field. After a rough start to the playoffs, it was a pretty sweet moment for the longest-tenured player with the Chicago Cubs.
item.dc:creator: JAY COHEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cubs, Brewers announce Game 4 starting pitchers
item.description: CHICAGO — The Chicago Cubs and the Milwaukee Brewers have announced their Game 4 starting pitchers. Lefty Matthew Boyd will toe the rubber for the North Siders and righty ace Freddy Peralta goes for the Brewers.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Cubs stave off elimination with 4-3 win over Brewers in Game 3 of NLDS
item.description: CHICAGO (AP/WGN) — The Chicago Cubs staved off elimination with a 4-3 win over the Milwaukee Brewers in Game 3 of the NLDS Wednesday night at Wrigley Field. Pete Crow-Armstrong's two-out swing was part of a four-run first inning for Chicago — continuing a wild trend.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Blake Snell shines on mound and Dodgers hold off Phillies 4-3 for 2-0 lead in NLDS
item.description: Blake Snell had yielded to the Los Angeles Dodgers' bullpen after a masterful start and was just a bystander when the Philadelphia Phillies — without any help from their All-Star trio at the top of the batting order — finally mustered a rally in the ninth inning.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Charges against former NFL QB Mark Sanchez upgraded amid investigation into stabbing incident
item.description: At a press conference on Monday, Marion County Prosecutor Ryan Mears filed more serious charges against former NFL quarterback Mark Sanchez for his involvement in a stabbing incident in downtown Indianapolis early Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Vickie Binkley, WGN 9

item.title: Julio Rodríguez lifts Mariners over Tigers 3-2, Seattle takes Game 2 of ALDS to even series
item.description: Rodríguez hit a tiebreaking RBI double in the eighth inning and the Mariners beat the Detroit Tigers 3-2 in Game 2 of their AL Division Series on Sunday to even the series.
item.dc:creator: DOUG MILLER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Broncos rally from 14-point deficit to hand Eagles their first loss of the season, 21-17
item.description: Bo Nix led the Broncos on three scoring drives on three possessions in the fourth quarter, J.K. Dobbins rushed for a 2-yard score in the period and the Broncos wiped out a 14-point deficit to hand the Philadelphia Eagles their first loss of the season, 21-17.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 49ers' Christian McCaffrey call San Francisco 'San Fran' in new commercial
item.description: (KRON) — 49ers running back and former Stanford University standout Christian McCaffrey committed a Bay Area cultural faux pas in a new Lowe’s ad posted to social media on Oct. 1. “What’s up, San Fran?” Christian McCaffrey says in the advertisement.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, KRON4

item.title: Cubs drop NLDS Game 1 to Brewers 9-3
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP/WGN) - The Chicago Cubs lost Game 1 of the NLDS to the Milwaukee Brewers Saturday afternoon at American Family Field by a score of 9-3. Jackson Chourio went 3 for 3 and drove in three runs in the first two innings before departing with a hamstring injury.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WGN 9

item.title: Noem: NFL ‘won’t be able to sleep at night’ over Bad Bunny Super Bowl performance
item.description: The NFL has faced blowback from conservative commentators and MAGA influencers over its decision to select Bad Bunny as the halftime headliner.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation
